hypothesis: Extending the closed-loop Bayesian optimization framework to simultaneously optimize a three-dimensional figure of merit that incorporates long-term electrode stability (quantified as the rate of reactor yield decay over extended electrolysis duration) alongside instantaneous CO partial current density and CO2 utilization will reveal that the operating conditions maximizing time-averaged reactor yield over multi-hour operation differ systematically from those identified by the five-minute electrolysis protocol used here, because the bicarbonate concentrations and current densities that maximize instantaneous reactor yield accelerate silver catalyst flooding and bipolar membrane degradation, creating a previously uncharacterized stability-performance trade-off that can only be resolved through autonomous multi-objective optimization incorporating temporal degradation data.
